issue_title: Capacity to change hibernate dialect
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Is not possible to change the dialect of Oracle Database

### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

Be capable to change the currrent dialect through the command above:
```
sudo /opt/keycloak-17.0.0/bin/kc.sh   build \
 --db=oracle \
--metrics-enabled=true  \
--cache-config-file=../conf/cache-ispn.xml  \
-Dkc.db-dialect=org.hibernate.dialect.Oracle12cDialect
```

### Actual behavior

The build above:
```
sudo /opt/keycloak-17.0.0/bin/kc.sh   build \
 --db=oracle \
--metrics-enabled=true  \
--cache-config-file=../conf/cache-ispn.xml  \
-Dkc.db-dialect=org.hibernate.dialect.Oracle12cDialect
```
Generate this config:
```
sudo /opt/keycloak-17.0.0/bin/kc.sh show-config
Runtime Configuration:
        kc.cache =  ispn (PersistedConfigSource)
        kc.cache-config-file =  ../conf/cache-ispn.xml (PersistedConfigSource)
        kc.config.args =  show-config (SysPropConfigSource)
        kc.db =  oracle (PersistedConfigSource)
        kc.db-dialect =  org.hibernate.dialect.Oracle12cDialect (PersistedConfigSource)
        kc.home.dir =  /opt/keycloak-17.0.0/bin/../ (SysPropConfigSource)
        kc.http-enabled =  false (PropertiesConfigSource[source=jar:file:///opt/keycloak-17.0.0/lib/lib/main/org.keycloak.keycloak-quarkus-server-17.0.0.jar!/META-INF/keycloak.conf])
        kc.http-relative-path =  / (PersistedConfigSource)
        kc.metrics-enabled =  true (PersistedConfigSource)
        kc.quarkus-properties-enabled =  true (PersistedConfigSource)
        kc.show.config =  none (SysPropConfigSource)
        kc.version =  17.0.0 (SysPropConfigSource)
```
But the defined dialect is not aplied, is possible to confirm the bug during startup time through the presence of an LIMIT clause in the above query 


```
Caused by: Error : 933, Position : 188, Sql = select migrationm0_.ID as id1_39_, migrationm0_.UPDATE_TIME as update_t2_39_, migrationm0_.VERSION as version3_39_ from MIGRATION_MODEL migrationm0_ order by migrationm0_.UPDATE_TIME DESC limit :1 , OriginalSql = select migrationm0_.ID as id1_39_, migrationm0_.UPDATE_TIME as update_t2_39_, migrationm0_.VERSION as version3_39_ from MIGRATION_MODEL migrationm0_ order by migrationm0_.UPDATE_TIME DESC limit ?, Error Msg = ORA-00933: SQL command not properly ended
```




### How to Reproduce?

Building the code through the command above 

```
sudo /opt/keycloak-17.0.0/bin/kc.sh  build \
--db=oracle \
--metrics-enabled=true\
--cache-config-file=../conf/cache-ispn.xml \
-Dkc.db-dialect=org.hibernate.dialect.Oracle12cDialect

```
And runing with an oracle database


### Anything else?

 i havent tested with other dialects and databases yet

issue_title: Replace operation set wrong lifespan in remote infinispan database and leads to session eviction
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Hi,

This discussion describes the bug and leads to fix it : https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/10577 

**To sum up here :** new session lifespan gets corrupted in database (set in past) when trying to refresh it and session entry is evicted from Infinispan database


**Technical stack :** 
Keycloak 17.0.0 (legacy, wildfly) (will be migrated to Quarkus in a few months)
Infinispan 13.0.6.Final with JDBC persistence (PostgreSQL 14.X)

Number of nodes in cluster does not matter.

**Note** : this bug does not occur with a remote Infinispan cluster without JDBC persistence.

### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

When a remote-store is configured pointing to an external Infinispan cluster with JDBC persistence, the following update must be done for the `timestamp `field in Infinispan database  : 

1. Create a new Session on App A. In database, new lifespan value (`timestamp`) is set to :  _now () + SSO Session Max configured in Realm_
2. Navigate to another app App B of same realm (or even same app A in another tab).  Lifespan value (`timestamp`) should be updated to : _now() + SSO Session Max configured in Realm_


### Actual behavior

1. Create a new Session on App A. In database, new lifespan value (timestamp) is set to :  _now () + SSO Session Max configured in Realm_
2. Navigate to another app App B of same realm (or even same app A in another tab).  Lifespan value (timestamp) is updated to : _Unix timestamp (01/01/1970 ) + SSO Session Max configured in Realm_
3. As a consequence, because this lifespan is in the past, User session is evicted quickly and user is logged out


### How to Reproduce?

Full example here : https://github.com/olivierboudet/keycloak-infinispan-jdbc 



### Anything else?

Lead from @thomasdarimont here : https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/10577#discussioncomment-2333456

Replace operation set wrong lifespan in remote infinispan database and leads to session eviction (#15619)

Closes #10755

issue_title: Attestation Conveyance Verification with YubiKey
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

# Disclaimer

This might be a bug, or might be a fixed issue from a previous version, which now is causing the error. The questioning behind the classification is explained in the description of the possible bug.

# Description

When trying to use Direct as Attestation Conveyance, keycloak reports an error (described in the actual behaviour). On previous version of keycloak (15.0), this error did not happen.

# References

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-community/blob/main/design/web-authn-authenticator.md
https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-11372
https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_installation/index.html#_truststore
https://developers.yubico.com/yubico-piv-tool/Attestation.html
https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn/#attestation-statement

# Considerations

We are using a physical YubiKey.
Using Chrome

# General Steps

This steps are required in both the expected behaviour and the actual behaviour. To avoid repetition, they are included in this section.

1. Enable WebAuthn Verification following the steps defined in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-documentation/blob/main/server_admin/topics/authentication/webauthn.adoc
2. Under Authentication > WebAuthn Policy > Attestation Conveyance Preference set it to Direct
3. Acceptable AAGUID's > the AAGUID's of the accepted YubiKeys.

### Version

17 (Quarkus)

### Expected behavior

When the Attestation Conveyance Preference is set to direct, according to the [WebAuthn Stadard](https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn/), we expect Keycloak to verify the Attestation provided by the Authentication / Browser and register the key information.

Steps:

1. User goes to login page
2. User is presented with username and password (first login)
3. User is required to register USB key to keycloak as specified in the General Steps
4. User registers key successfully and keycloak saves the key information on the database
5. User proceeds to initially intended web page

issue_title: Keycloak is not fully supporting XML boolean values for SAML
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

As described in [w3c XML Schema definition](https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema11-2/#boolean), XML boolean values can be false/true but also can be 0/1.
More specifically, when reading a SAML document coming from a client who is encoding boolean values with 0 or 1, both values are recognized as false in Keycloak since StaxParserUtil::getBooleanAttributeValue is evaluated with: `return value == null ? null : Boolean.valueOf(value)`

For clients who are supporting 0/1 as boolean values, Keycloak can't process SAML correctly.

Should we submit a PR?

### Version

At least from 14.0.0 to master

### Expected behavior

When receiving SAML statements, Keycloak should understand that 1 is a true boolean value

### Actual behavior

When receiving SAML statements, Keycloak considers legitimate value 1 as false

### How to Reproduce?

Use a client who is using 0/1 as boolean values.
Ex: <saml2p:NameIDPolicy AllowCreate="1" />

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Support 0/1 values for XML boolean attributes

Closes #10802

issue_title: Re-augmentation is recording runtime properties as runtime default values
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

During augmentation, Quarkus resolves runtime properties used during the augmentation (e.g.: `build`) (other than env variables) and set them as runtime default values. These properties are recorded and available from a generated config source called "specified runtime default values".

Because of that, when you run `start-dev` for the very first time:

```
kc.sh start-dev --proxy=edge
```

And then you run again the same command without the `--proxy` option:

```
kc.sh start-dev
```

The value `edge` is still set at runtime even though no longer available from the command line.

We should remove any runtime option when running the build command and particularly when running `auto-build` so that Quarkus does not record these options and does not use them as runtime defaults.


### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

Any runtime option set when running `auto-build` is not recorded and can be reverted to the default when starting the server.

### Actual behavior

Runtime options do not revert to their default values if not set after re-augmentation.

### How to Reproduce?

Run `start-dev` for the very first time to force a `build`:

```
kc.sh start-dev --proxy edge
```

Now start the server without passing the `proxy` option:

```
kc.sh start
```

Check if the `proxy` option is set to `edge`. It should not but set to `none`.

### Anything else?

As a workaround, you can run `kc.sh build` without passing any argument to "reset" the server state.

issue_title: keycloak-common module has Java 1.8 API calls while the module is set to use Java 1.7
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/10334 introduces some API calls that are [available in Java 1.8](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/lang/reflect/Method.html) but [not in 1.7](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/reflect/Method.html). The module target version [is 1.7](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/92c4e6d585dca4d2f741ac4ac6b50ea0c0940a9a/common/pom.xml#L35-L36).

### Version

### Expected behavior

```
/Users/vmuzikar/Documents/keycloak/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/common/util/reflections/Types.java:166:19
java: cannot find symbol
  symbol:   method getParameterCount()
  location: variable method of type java.lang.reflect.Method
/Users/vmuzikar/Documents/keycloak/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/common/util/reflections/Types.java:166:53
java: cannot find symbol
  symbol:   method getParameterCount()
  location: variable intfMethod of type java.lang.reflect.Method
/Users/vmuzikar/Documents/keycloak/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/common/util/reflections/Types.java:168:35
java: cannot find symbol
  symbol:   method getParameterCount()
  location: variable method of type java.lang.reflect.Method
```

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

Build the project in your IDE. Maven seems to be ok with it for some reason, so it's not reproducible with it.

### Anything else?

Workaround for IntelliJ IDEA: override the target bytecode version for "keycloam-common" module in the IDEA Settings --> Build, Execution, Deployment --> Compiler --> Java Compiler
This works, at least until next POMs re-import when this will get again set to what's in the POM.

issue_title: Maven builds doesn't recognize JDK 1.7 API violations
issue_body:
### Description

The Maven builds runs with JDK 11, and some modules still claim want to bei JDK 7 compatible. 

By using JDK 11 to build, it misses when Java adds a new API in JDK 8-11: The code still compiles, still it will fail when running on JDK 7. 

To prevent this, Keycloak should apply a tool like https://www.mojohaus.org/animal-sniffer/ in its build process. The build will then fail when an API is used that is too recent.

At the same time: The Keycloak documentation states OpenJDK 8 should be used, and Maven compiles to 1.7. Strange.

### Motivation

Prevent a situation as it happend in #10840.
commit_message:
Ensure that only JDK 8 APIs are used where JDK 8 is still required.

Closes #10842

issue_title: SAML Adapters tests start failing in recent versions of EAP/wildfly
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

[ELYWEB-163](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/ELYWEB-163) fixed a bug in elytron-web that re-encodes (twice) the query parameters. It seems that OIDC/SAML did a workaround to fix this just re-decoding the values also two times ([here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/15.0.2/adapters/oidc/wildfly-elytron/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/elytron/ElytronHttpFacade.java#L200-L218), first `getQuery` do one decoding and after that the code perform the second one). My feeling that this was not even a workaround, it just worked and no one thought about being doing a first decoding calling `getQuery` (`getRawQuery` in URI returns the query string without deconding, but calling `getQuery` it is decoded).

After the fix the double decoding makes the signature parameter in SAML REDIRECT binding fail (it is wrong after the second decoding).

More info: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHSSO-1943

### Version

17.0.0 15.0.2

It's weird but probably we need to make it work in both situations (with and without the fix). Because if not the adapters will fail when installed in a version without ELYWEB-163 applied.

issue_title: [Docs] conflict between best practices regarding the JavaScript adapter
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The [JavaScript adapter's documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/securing_apps/#_javascript_adapter) recommends serving the adapter from the Keycloak server:

> A **best practice** is to load the JavaScript adapter **directly from Keycloak Server** as it will automatically be updated when you upgrade the server. If you copy the adapter to your web application instead, make sure you upgrade the adapter only after you have upgraded the server.

On the contrary, the Keycloak guide "Using a reverse proxy" recommends in its paragraph ["Exposed path recommendations"](https://www.keycloak.org/server/reverseproxy#_exposed_path_recommendations) to **not** expose the Keycloak `/js` path, and serve the JS adapter from the client:

> A **good practice** is to not use external js for the javascript client, but bake it into your public client instead.

This is contradictory. Of course, both options are possible. But Keycloak should recommend only one (with proper documentation) as a *best practice*, or just expose the pros/cons for each option.

### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

N/A

### Actual behavior

N∕A

### How to Reproduce?

N/A

### Anything else?

N/A

issue_title: Getting 404 not found when using templates in custom provider deployed under providers folder
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I have two providers I use one from `[GitHub - BenjaminFavre/keycloak-apple-social-identity-provider: Apple Social Identity Provider for Keycloak](https://github.com/BenjaminFavre/keycloak-apple-social-identity-provider)`, and I wrote another custom provider. 

The custom templates are housed under **`myprovider.jar\theme-resources\resources\partials`**, just as they should be packaged.

Bear in mind I do run `kc.bat build` after placing both the jar files in the **`keycloak-17.0.0\providers`** folder.

When editing configured providers using say following links below I get 404 on one of them unless I remove one or the other, or place the template files in **`keycloak-17.0.0\themes\base\admin\resources\partials`** to prevent **404** message

*/admin/master/console/#/realms/idp/identity-provider-settings/provider/apple/apple*

*/admin/master/console/#/realms/idp/identity-provider-settings/provider/myprovider/myprovider*

In Keycloak version 15.0.0 when browsing to **`/admin/master/console/#/server-info/providers`** under section I see

```
themeResource	 apple-social-identity-provider-1.0.2.jar
                                 myprovider.jar
```
In Keycloak version 17.0.0 when browsing to `/admin/master/console/#/server-info/providers` under section I see
```
themeResource	classpath
```

NOTE: This works flawlessly in version 15.0.0, and I able to edit both providers using custom templates bundled in jar files.

### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

It should find the template bundled in the jar file and allow updating custom provider settings.

### Actual behavior

I get 404 when editing/creating one of the provider's settings that were deployed.

### How to Reproduce?

1.  Download and extract Keycloak https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/download/17.0.0/keycloak-17.0.0.tar.gz
2.  Download custom identity provider for Apple sign in
https://github.com/BenjaminFavre/keycloak-apple-social-identity-provider/releases/download/v1.0.2/apple-social-identity-provider-1.0.2.jar and copy to **`[KEYCLOAK-17.0.0-PATH]\providers`**
3. Create another custom provider similar to  `https://github.com/BenjaminFavre/keycloak-apple-social-identity-provider` build the jar file and drop it in **`[KEYCLOAK-17.0.0]\providers`**
4. Run **`[KEYCLOAK-17.0.0-PATH]\bin\kc.bat build`**
5. Run **`[KEYCLOAK-17.0.0-PATH]\bin\kc.bat start`**
6. Create a new Realm **`idp`**
7. Browse to **`[YOUR-KEYCLOAK-HOST]/admin/master/console/#/realms/idp/identity-provider-settings`**, add your custom identity provider from the drop-down list. You will get a 404 when attempting to add one of the custom providers, and not the other.

### Anything else?

issue_title: Client ID in LDAP Mappers User Federation doesn't align with Rename Client ID
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Scenario lead the bug/error:
1. Create LDAP mapper type `role-type-mapper` with name `foo-client-ldap-mapper` in User Federation menu Keycloak Admin Console.
2. Disable option `Use Realm Roles Mapping`.
3. Select `Client ID` field to integrate LDAP mapper (e.g. `foo-client`).
4. Save and sync Roles from LDAP to Keycloak.
5. Go to Clients menu and rename `Client ID` from `foo-client` to `bar-client`.
6. Check in `foo-client-ldap-mapper` form then `Client ID` field empty because the `Client ID` was renamed.
7. When user login (e.g. test with `account` or `account-console` Client ID) it throws error `Unexpected error when handling authentication request to identity provider.`

**Current solution**
Restore name of Client ID as before then it works.

### Version

17.0.0 Quarkus Distribution

### Expected behavior
`Client ID` field in `foo-client-ldap-mapper` should be align with renamed client or save by `id` of client instead of `clientId`.

### Actual behavior

`Client ID` field in `foo-client-ldap-mapper` still shows renamed `Client ID`. I assume that the way Keycloak save the `Client ID` in `foo-client-ldap-mapper` based on `clientId` not `id` of client. So, that's why it leads error `Unexpected error when handling authentication request to identity provider.`

### How to Reproduce?

Already describe in section **Describe the Bug**

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Keycloak "forgets" ui_locales parameter when using Reset Password functionality
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When the user is redirected back to the login page after entering their email in the "Forgot password" dialog, the language resets to the realm's default language, rather than using a previously set ui_locales parameter.

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

When being sent back to the login page after a reset password flow, the page should still be translated in the language that was requested via the ui_locales parameter.

### Actual behavior

Instead, the language is reset to the default language of the selected realm.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a new realm and client with the authorization code flow, in this case, both named "test". Set the client's redirect_uri to "*".
2. Enable "forgot password" on the realm
3. Enable "Localization" on the realm, and select at least 2 languages (in this case, de and en, with the default language being de)
4. Navigate to [http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=test&response_type=code&redirect_uri=https://google.com&ui_locales=en](http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=test&response_type=code&redirect_uri=https://google.com&ui_locales=en)
5. Click "Forgot Password?" and enter any email (an actual user is not required to exist)
6. After submitting the email, the language of the form will have switched to German, rather than the requested English

### Anything else?

When using the language switcher in the login form to switch the language to english, keycloak redirects the user and appends the kc_locale parameter. This parameter is correctly remembered, and even after a password reset, when the user gets redirected back to the login page, the page is in the expected language.

As a workaround, we tried appending kc_locale directly to the auth URL, like [http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=test&response_type=code&redirect_uri=https://google.com&ui_locales=en&kc_locale=en](http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=test&response_type=code&redirect_uri=https://google.com&ui_locales=en&kc_locale=en), as well as [http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=test&response_type=code&redirect_uri=https://google.com&kc_locale=en](http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=test&response_type=code&redirect_uri=https://google.com&kc_locale=en), but both of these seem to be ignored. They are only used when the user manually selects a language using the language selector.
commit_message:
Keycloak forgets ui_locales parameter when using reset password
closes #10981

issue_title: SAML Client does not validate IdP Issuer value
issue_body:
### Description

When receiving assertions from a remote IdP, the client should verify both that the signature is correct and that the claimed issuer is the expected one. At the moment, Keycloak SAML IdP configuration does not have an attribute to identify the remote IdP issuer and does not perform issuer validation.
Also, when importing a SAML IdP from metadata, the expected issuer can be automatically populated from the metadata document.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Keycloak should validate that the SAML Issuer is set to the expected value in addition to the other already present checks, otherwise it should return a Bad Request error.

For retrocompatibility, if the expected Issuer is not set it should accept any Issuer in SAML responses.

### Details

I will provide a PR for this feature soon.
commit_message: Fix #10982 SAML Client - Introduce SAML Issuer validation

issue_body:
### Description

Currently, for case-insensitive search and order we have some fields doubled, e.g.:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/19e55650a20592e843485cc79dc648ddea24a56c/model/map-hot-rod/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/storage/hotRod/user/HotRodUserEntity.java#L64-L70

The cause is that by using the analyzed index, we achieve case-insensitivity but we lose the possibility to order because the analyzed index doesn't know the original value.

We should be able to replace the doubled field with a doubled index (we should be able to use two annotations on the same field) which should end up with fewer data in the store.

issue_title: Allow overriding the host header using X-Forwarded-Host
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I run a httpd server as reverse proxy upfront a keycloak. The TTLs termination is done by the httpd server. In order to pass the incoming requests to keycloak-node we use a secured connection via https with a self signed CA which is setup for the whole internal backend infrastructure. 

The [Proxy-Userguide](https://www.keycloak.org/server/reverseproxy) expects a preserved Host header, which is not possible since this will fail the certificate check during the proxy action.

If i do not preserve the Host to keycloak and navigate to to the admin-console the browser wants to load a resource which is of the same origin of the Host-Header (which is internal keycloak adress) not the X-Forwarded-For value. The admin console cannot be loaded because the browser cannot directly load anything from the internal backend network.

`curl -v  https://somepublic.domain.com/auth/admin/master/console/`
returns a html-response which contains a script resource with url **stage-kc**
`<script src="https://stage-kc/auth/js/keycloak.js?version=dvfah" type="text/javascript"></script>`

Same for authUrl of the master / console => this is an internal address, this should either be not relevant or exposed to public.

```
 <script type="text/javascript">
        var authServerUrl = 'https://somepublic.domain.com/auth';
        var authUrl = 'https://stage-kc/auth';
        var consoleBaseUrl = '/auth/admin/master/console/';
        var resourceUrl = '/auth/resources/dvfah/admin/keycloak';
        var masterRealm = 'master';
        var resourceVersion = 'dvfah';
    </script>
```

I also tried with the feature admin2, which also fails loading and even requests calls to

http://127.0.0.1:8080/auth/admin/master/console/whoami
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/701101/160783145-ca787c0f-f335-46c5-bc4c-9777cfdc9caf.png)


### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

The UI resources urls respect the set X-Forwarded-For header and do not rely on the Host header.

should return a public facing url
`<script src="https://somepublic.domain.com/auth/js/keycloak.js?version=dvfah" type="text/javascript"></script>`

### Actual behavior

Do not rely on the Host-Header for url-path. Rather use the X-Forwarded-* header values.



### How to Reproduce?

**apache httpd config**

 ```
<Location /auth>
                ProxyPass            https://STAGE-KC:8343/auth
                ProxyPassReverse     https://STAGE-KC:8343/auth
                ProxyPassReverseCookieDomain  "STAGE-KC"  "somepublic.domain.com"
                RequestHeader set X-Forwarded-Proto "https"
                RequestHeader set X-Forwarded-Port "443"
                RequestHeader set X-Forwarding-For "somepublic.domain.com"
                RequestHeader set X-Forwarded-For "somepublic.domain.com"
                RequestHeader set X-Forwarded-Host "somepublic.domain.com"
     </Location>
```

**keycloak.conf**
```
proxy=reencrypt
hostname=stage-kc
http-enabled=true
http-host=0.0.0.0
http-port=8383
https-port=8343
http-relative-path=/auth

spi-x509cert-lookup-apache-ssl-client-cert=SSL_CLIENT_CERT
spi-x509cert-lookup-apache-ssl-cert-chain-prefix=CERT_CHAIN
spi-x509cert-lookup-apache-certificate-chain-length=2
```

**keycloak build**
I used `--spi-x509cert-lookup-provider=apache` for the upfront keycloak build.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Allow overriding the host header using X-Forwarded-Host (#11021)

Closes #10997

issue_title: Script Mapper Performance Issues - Java 8 and Java 11
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

While switching from a Java 8 based deployment to Java 11 of RHSSO, we noticed an increase in memory usage and that some objects were long lived.

Our RHSSO deployment makes use of script mappers. We ultimately have determined that the Nashorn engine in Java 11 is creating many weak/soft references and are not culled with mixed GC as they were with Java 8. This ultimately results in the application being required to perform a Full GC or otherwise risk memory exhaustion.

There are also some other changes to Nashorn in Java 11 that also create additional classloaders and classes with each instantiation of the engine (a factor of 3).

Additionally, we determined this is also an issue to some degree with Java 8 - it went unnoticed previously as the Full GC cycles did not occur (Java 8 was able to reclaim the space during mixed gc), but could be observed by the rather fast allocation and promotion of new objects from young to old gen, but subsequently cleared by mixed GC. By turning off the script mappers, we could observe the young and old gen growth rates slowed significantly.

There are some recommendations about sharing the ScriptEngine created initially (I believe RHSSO/Keycloak creates it with each request), which could reduce some of the resource consumption. I am unsure if that would solve the weak/soft reference issues in Java 11 as those could be from one of many bugs open for Java 11 + Nashorn.


### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

- Run RHSSO on Java 11 (can use Openshift Image) with G1GC
- Ensure scripts and upload scripts feature is enabled
- Create a client with a script mapper - contents can be very simple like
```
 token.audience("some-other-aud"); exports = true;
```
- Run load test to generate access , ID , refresh tokens against the endpoint
- Monitor heap usage and watch Old Gen growth and Eden creation rates.



### Anything else?

_No response_

Closes #11005

issue_title: Account console not functional when running KeyCloak behind a reverse proxy
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I just setup my first install of KeyCloak, following the various guides in the documentation, but I have run into a problem (for which I also have the solution)

The problem is, then I try and access the account console on https://keycloak-server/realms/testrealm/account/ I get the "Account console is loading" going on forever.

If I check the Network tab, I can see that I get a 403 when trying to get keycloak.js, which is understandable, because I set it up that way based on the guide found here, https://www.keycloak.org/server/reverseproxy (I have a few requires in place in my apache conf to limit the paths that should not be exposed publicly)

When I access the page from a privileged IP that is not limited by apache, I can access the account console just fine.

So, my question is, is the guide wrong?  And if it isn't, how am I going to allow users to change their account related settings?

It does seem strange that if I run KeyCloak behind a proxy, I should limit some folders, but if I run it on a dedicated server, everything is fine.

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

A user accessing the account console from the public side of the reverse proxy can actually access it.

### Actual behavior

User can not access the account console 

### How to Reproduce?

Follow the reverse proxy setup guide, and try and access the account console.

### Anything else?

The fix is pretty clear, allow access to the /js/ folder, but someone wrote that guide for a reason, and I don't want to open up for potential security risks by ignoring it.

issue_title: Testsuite failures when JPA Map Storage is used for realms
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The following tests fail when the base testsuite is run with the JPA Map Storage for realms is enabled:

- org.keycloak.testsuite.admin.ComponentsTest.txt (2 test failures)
- org.keycloak.testsuite.federation.ldap.LDAPRoleMappingsTest.txt  (1 test failure)

The root issue is the same:
```
org.keycloak.models.ModelException: javax.persistence.OptimisticLockException: Row was updated or deleted by another transaction (or unsaved-value mapping was incorrect) : [org.keycloak.models.map.storage.jpa.realm.entity.JpaRealmEntity#c248659e-825e-4b3e-b8b9-110ed4235a6f]
```

### Version

Unreleased

### Expected behavior

Testsuite must run without errors when the JPA Map Storage is enabled for all areas

### Actual behavior

Tests in the description fail with `OptimisticLockException`

### How to Reproduce?

1- Setup PostgreSQL according to instruction in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/9596
2- Run the tests enabling the JPA map storage for realms using the correct parameters:

```
mvn clean install -Dkeycloak.profile.feature.map_storage=enabled -Dkeycloak.realm.provider=map \
-Dkeycloak.realm.map.storage.provider=jpa-map-storage -Dkeycloak.map.storage.connectionsJpa.url=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/keycloak \
```

issue_title: SLO and ACS Binding are linked with AuthnRequest Binding in SAML Identity Broker Metadata
issue_body:
### Describe the bug Keycloak has three settings whether HTTP-POST Binding is used or not in SAML Identity Broker. - HTTP-POST Binding Response (postBindingResponse) - HTTP-POST Binding for AuthnRequest (postBindingAuthnRequest) - HTTP-POST Binding Logout (postBindingLogout) But these settings are not linked correctly in the SP Metadata Binding . PR #11080 ### Version 17.0.1 ### Expected behavior | Keycloak Setting Name | Keycloak Setting Value | SP Metadata Binding | | ---- | ---- | ---- | | postBindingAuthnRequest | false | - | | postBindingResponse | true | AssertionConsumerService
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-POST | | postBindingLogout | true | SingleLogoutService
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-POST | ```xml urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:persistent ``` ### Actual behavior | Keycloak Setting Name | Keycloak Setting Value | SP Metadata Binding | | ---- | ---- | ---- | | postBindingAuthnRequest | false | - | | postBindingResponse | true | AssertionConsumerService
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-Redirect | | postBindingLogout | true | SingleLogoutService
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-Redirect | ```xml urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:persistent ``` ### How to Reproduce? 1. Create SAML Identity Provider 2. HTTP-POST Binding Response Setting ON 3. HTTP-POST Binding Logout Setting ON 4. Save 5. Check SAML Identity Provider Metadata ### Anything else? I found the another problem. When the setting of postBindingLogout is null, postBindingLogout is linked with the setting of postBindingLogout. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/bfce612641a70e106b20b136431f0e4046b5c37f/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLIdentityProviderConfig.java#L222-L230 HTTP-POST Binding Logout default value is OFF in admin console. Therefore appearance of admin console and actual behavior do not match. I think that HTTP-POST Binding Logout default is `ON` in appearance of new admin cosole.

SLO and ACS Binding are linked with AuthnRequest Binding in SAML Identity Broker Metadata

Closes #11079

issue_title: Blank admin console page in Keycloak 17.0.1
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Having set the port to be 443 using

```
# Use port 443
https-port=443
```

in the config file, the initial Keycloak page loads, then click "Administration Console", log in and then you are presented with a blank admin console page, with just the black header at the top.

This is not the case if port 443 is not specified. The initial page is reachable at keycloak.mydomain.com:8443, click admin console link, log in and admin console is ok.

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

Expect to see all the contents of the admin console as when using the default 8443 port.

### Actual behavior

Everything below the header is empty. In addition, clicking on the user "Admin" in the top right of the header and attempting to go to Manage Account fails.

### How to Reproduce?

You can pretty much follow these simple instructions and then move on to part 4 where port 443 is enabled:
https://www.soundsessential.com/blog/214-keycloak-17-filemaker-installation-configuration-tutorial-part-3-preparing-keycloak-17

Note, do not add the port number to the hostname for 17.0.1 in the config file in part 3 above.

It's pretty straightforward with MySQL, SSL, install Keycloak, set up config file (NOTE: NO PROXY IS SET UP), start Keycloak to test, shut it down, change port to 443, rebuild, reboot, start Keycloak, log in and error is encountered. 

It's built on Ubuntu 20.

### Anything else?

I have also been seeing some errors generated when initially logging in, such as:

`2022-04-02 14:54:45,722 WARN  [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-0) KC-SERVICES0013: Failed authentication: java.lang.RuntimeException: java.io.IOException: Underlying input stream returned zero bytes`

I need to test further to see if these issues are related but adding here in case this means more to someone in the know!

If any additional details or testing is needed, happy to help so feel free to ask.

issue_title: Securing credentials/passwords not possible with Quarkus distribution
issue_body:
Keycloak should provide integration with SmallRye Keystore. Each config option should support values from a Java Keystore.

Currently blocked by https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-config/pull/833

---

### Describe the bug

It seems there is currently no way to secure/hide/obfuscate any of the passwords (i.e. db-password, https-key-store-password, https-trust-store-password, referenced in https://www.keycloak.org/server/all-config).

With Wildfly one could use Elytron encrypted values and "credential-stores".

This is an issue because, as @FX-Liagre points out in the referenced discussions

> [...]And our customers' security guys (and DB admins...) tend to faint when you present them a config text file with clear text DB credentials... :-(

### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

Have some sort of encryption ready for hiding credentials or passwords.

### Actual behavior

No such feature available in Quarkus, but still is in Wildfly.

### How to Reproduce?

Provide any clear-text passwords inside the `keycloak.conf`.
Find someone who does a security scan on the installation -> they will find the clear-text password and might possibly faint over it

### Anything else?

See also Discussion:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/10595

_No response_

Remove SOAPException from SOAPBindingTest as RunOnServer cannot load it

issue_title: keycloak-core module is using Java 1.8 class while it is set to use Java 1.7
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

StaxParserUtil is using `java.util.function.BiFunction`, however, it should not as the module is set to use Java 1.7.

See:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/395bd447f2d5e05254d6150cc720874d9452fea4/saml-core/src/test/java/org/keycloak/saml/common/util/StaxParserUtilTest.java#L25

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

Workaround for IntelliJ IDEA: override the target bytecode version for "keycloam-common" module in the IDEA Settings --> Build, Execution, Deployment --> Compiler --> Java Compiler
This works, at least until next POMs re-import when this will get again set to what's in the POM.
commit_message:
Remove usage of BiFunction from keycloak-core module

Closes #11091

issue_title: Do not empty attributes if they are not provided when user profile is enabled
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When the user declarative profile feature is enabled, attributes that are defined in the User Profile are always present in the user attributes even if they are not set (Admin Panel -> Users -> user -> attibutes tab). 

Also, on an attempt to update a user through PUT endpoint attributes are removed from the user.

### Context
We are using ABAC model and make use of the Keycloak user attributes extensively. To prevent users from accessing areas they should not have access to by modifying their attributes we enabled the Declarative User Profile feature and disabled write access to all the important attributes for the users leavin read access only (via Admin Panel -> Realm settings -> User Profile tab -> Attributes subtab ->  attribute -> Permissions section -> Can user edit = FALSE,  Can user view = TRUE).
At the same time, administrators should have read and write access for those attributes (via Admin Panel -> Realm settings -> User Profile tab -> Attributes subtab ->  attribute -> Permissions section -> Can admin edit = TRUE,  Can admin view = TRUE). Attributes are not required in our case.

#### Problem 1
But when this is configured in this way, administrators can see attributes with empty values in the user attributes page (Admin Panel -> Users -> user -> Attributes tab) even if the attribute has not been set for the user. This is confusing for the administrators.

#### Problem 2
We make use of the Admin REST API to make some modifications on the users, for example, update email. But to make it happen it's necessary to send the whole user representation with all the attributes otherwise they will be removed. This makes it necessary to do GET user request first to get full user representation modify it by updating email address and send PUT request to make an update. This introduces unneccessary HTTP call that could be avoided (and can be avoided in case Declarative User Profile feature is disabled)


### Version

16.1.1

### Expected behavior

1. Attributes that are not set for a user are not shown in the Admin Panel -> Users -> user -> Attributes tab
2. On a request to the `PUT /auth/admin/realms/{realm}/users/{id}` endpoint without `attributes` field in the request body all existing user attributes must preserve as it works when User Declarative Profile Feature is disabled.

### Actual behavior

1. For users without attributes from the User Profile configuration fields are still shown on the user attributes tab in the Admin panel with an empty value
2. On a request to the `PUT /auth/admin/realms/{realm}/users/{id}` endpoint without `attributes` field in the request body all existing user attributes are removed when User Declarative Profile Feature is disabled.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Enable Declarative User Profile feature https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#enabling-the-user-profile
2. Create a user with username `test`, email `test@test.com`, and **without any attributes**
3. Go to Admin Panel -> Realm settings -> User Profile tab -> Attributes subtab ->  click Create 
4. Create a new attribute with the following parameters and click save:
    a. name: test-attribute
    b. Required: FALSE
    c. Permissions -> Can admin edit: TRUE
    d. Permissions -> Can admin view = TRUE
    e. Permissions -> Can user edit: FALSE
    f. Permissions -> Can user view = TRUE    
5. Go to the user created on the Step 2 Admin Panel -> Users -> user with username `test` (from Step 2) -> Attributes tab
    a. `test-attribute` is shown with empty value even though it's not set for the user. (Actual behavior 1)
6. Set user attribute `test-attribute` value to `Test Value` and save the user
7. Make a PUT request to the `PUT /auth/admin/realms/{realm}/users/{id}` endpoint to update user email for the user created on the step 2. Payload: 
```json
{
```

issue_title: Document usage of hostname has to comply with standards
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Follow-up of #10930 (see latest comment) 

At the moment, we're not checking the value for `hostname` for validity. These checks are also not done by quarkus itself. Validity (as of RFC 952/1123 etc., see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hostname#Syntax) is needed for keycloak to work correctly. 

### Version

current

### Expected behavior

~~all hostnames are converted to lowercase first, and then checked for general validity. If valid, they should be used, if not, an error should be shown.~~

"how to" for adding the hostname value is added to hostname guide ( see #11139 for details on changing)
### Actual behavior

Keycloak/Quarkus just accepts invalid hostname values like `aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa` (more than 253 chars).
 

### How to Reproduce?

Run e.g. `./kc.sh start --hostname=LOCALHOST --https-certificate-file ../conf/id.keycloak.test.pem --https-certificate-key-file ../conf/id.keycloak.test-key.pem`, create an initial admin user and login to admin console. The admin console will not show up apart from the header menu (see e.g. #10930)  , as the hostname is not standard compliant.

### Anything else?

-
commit_message:
Add section about hostname syntax to hostname guide

Superseeds PR #11139, as validation needs more investigation to work for everyone

Closes #11134

issue_title: Event for MISSING_REQUIRED_DESTINATION with idp brokering incorrectly says error is related to logout even for a login response
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When the brokered identity provider returns a signed saml:Response document without a Destination attribute a error event is generated with "error" being "invalid_logout_response" when this is a Response to a AuthnRequest and not a logout.




### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

event should contain error=invalid_saml_response or an input dependent error (invalid_logout_response in the case of LogoutResponse and invalid_saml_response in the case of Response).

For example, 

`TRACE [org.keycloak.events] (default task-539) type=IDENTITY_PROVIDER_RESPONSE_ERROR, realmId=myrealm, clientId=null, userId=null, ipAddress=xx.xx.xx.xx, error=invalid_saml_response, reason=missing_required_destination, requestUri=...`


### Actual behavior

Event logged shows:

`TRACE [org.keycloak.events] (default task-539) type=IDENTITY_PROVIDER_RESPONSE_ERROR, realmId=myrealm, clientId=null, userId=null, ipAddress=xx.xx.xx.xx, error=invalid_logout_response, reason=missing_required_destination, requestUri=...`
